focus: Driver hatred, range vs course disconnect, mental blocks, confidence issues, love/hate relationship

PAIN.quote: The title says it all. And it isn't the specific club, I am not trying to come up with a reason to buy a new one. I just can't hit it. All I do is hit big pull slices with my driver
I can tee off with my 5w and 3w just fine. Can even hit push draws with my 5w, but the driver is a complete crap shoot
PAIN.context: Other clubs work fine - driver fails

PAIN.quote: I hit my driver on trackman indoors... goes fine, feels fine. Get outside... FOV is much wider and the target is much further away and suddenly I can't hit it
I hate my driver with a passion. I think it is all about confidence
I seem to have this inability to use the driver, 3 wood or hybrid on the course and this is affecting my chances of lowering my handicap

The problem is that on the range, my driver works well... At The Shire, I had a really good practise/warm up session. was relaxed... And lets say that out of 10 drives on the range - 3 great, 5 okay and 2 poor. So I thought lets use the club - felt confident etc. The 3rd hole was the first hole to use it on and I shanked it
I just seem to have this... thing...happen when I get halfway up my driver backswing and it all goes to pot from there on in - there is an eagerness to hit the ball, see where it goes

My driver is killing me... I'm slightly bottoming out & hitting the turf first (probably 50% of the time). I can't figure out how I can even hit the ground when the tee'd ball is practically 3 inches above the turf

HOPE.quote: I had a similar love hate relationship with driver. When it was good it was very good, but the bad ones were so destructive. I did what any sensible chap would do...bought a 3 wood and used it exclusively off the tee. Handicap fell 4 shots in a little over 2 months

I have had clubs like this that I just couldn't hit. A new club gives you a reset. Most will tell you this is the wrong advice and it probably is but it worked for me. 3 wood was never touched in a round because I didn't trusted. Trade in and 90 quid later, it's my go to club

a friend told me 'don't hit the ball, just swing the ferkin club' so that's what I do, couple of practice swing to get me eye in then setup behind the ball and just swing me club... you'll be very surprised at how well this little tactic works
HOPE.context: Don't hit ball, just swing = works

HOPE.quote: I now expect to hit it well, and even a mishit is not terrible now. Confidence breeds good repetition and you have to hit it lots to get there. I will add that shortening the shaft helped a lot

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The longer the club, the more it amplifies any swing faults you have
since it's also the longest (shafted) club and we're expected to hit the ball the farthest with it, it's easy to tense up with it, let the swing get too long, put a violent, bad swing on it
it's easy to think, for instance, that you need to swing more from the inside, only to find that part of your problem is swinging too inside already
Its a mental thing that I cant quite seem to conquer... Driving range - slow, smooth, specific targets, wide, etc. Course - swing it like a complete jerk!

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I'm having lessons, but as I said earlier, on the range or in the lessons I'm fine
I have even tried gripping down the shaft a little
I have moved the ball to the center of my stance instead of off the front foot to try to make contact at the bottom of my swing arc - doesn't seem to help much

He got maybe 2/3 drives out of 12 on a round. Other hits were duck hooks or high fades. Anyways he came up to American golf with me... What a turnaround with the ping forgiveness and having the regular shaft hes like a new golfer

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I can't see how getting a new club will help as its me swinging it poorly on the course which is the problem - lack of shoulder turn, sequence totally out, smashing the sh*t out of it etc. no point getting a £350 driver if I cant swing properly
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Recognizes new club won't fix swing

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: If driver is only club you have no confidence it, it's time for a new one, or lessons to overcome what sounds like a mental block. Have to love your driver and be confident with it
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Must love your driver - confidence key

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I doubt there is one tour pro playing the length of your driver (assuming it's stock as you didnt indicate otherwise). There are good reasons for that
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Stock drivers too long vs tour

VILLAIN.quote: When it was good it was very good, but the bad ones were so destructive, miss left, miss right, red arrows it certainly was. When you're coming out of trees sideways every second shot, golf gets boring
We also spoke about mental side of the game when standing on the tee with the big stick - Fear of failure, one shot only, striving for distance
since it's also the longest (shafted) club and we're expected to hit the ball the farthest with it, it's easy to tense up with it
Simple - you are only taking the driver when you need the distance on the course, and are swinging it to get more distance as a result
as the driver is such a longer club it's easy to have too much body and not enough arms, so get a bit OTT or draggy with the handle

high_value_themes_identified: I hate my driver with a PASSION
New club = mental reset (works for some)
Stock drivers too long vs tour pros
Don't hit ball - just swing club works
